stem: A clinical research study measures treatment response rate across 5 trial sites, each containing multiple patient cohorts. trial site A has 15 observations with mean 28.3 % (within-trial site SD = 5.0). trial site B has 5 observations with mean 44.9 % (within-trial site SD = 3.7). trial site C has 8 observations with mean 39.1 % (within-trial site SD = 7.1). trial site D has 5 observations with mean 42.8 % (within-trial site SD = 9.7). trial site E has 8 observations with mean 59.8 % (within-trial site SD = 6.8). The between-trial site standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 7.4 and 18.6 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true treatment response rate for trial site A, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-trial site variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for trial site A: 28.4 % (shrunk from raw 28.3). Range [28.4, 28.7] as between-trial site SD varies from 7.4 to 18.6. Pooled mean: 40.3.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 40.3 % (weighted average of 5 trial site means).
Shrinkage factor for trial site A: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/15). At σ_between=12.4: B = 0.989.
Interval [28.4, 28.7] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (28.3) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (40.3) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
